stem: Refer to the given figures (a) and (b). A logic analyzer is used to check the circuit in figure (a) and displays the waveforms shown in figure (b). The actual analyzer display shows all four data outputs, Q0-Q3. The analyzer's cursor is placed at position X and all four of the data output lines show a Low-level output. What is wrong, if anything, with the circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: Nothing is wrong, according to the display. The outputs are in the open state and should show zero output voltage.

options.label: B
options.text: The circuit is in the READ mode and the outputs, Q0-Q3, should reflect the contents of the memory at that address. The chip is defective; replace the chip.

options.label: C
options.text: The circuit is in the      mode and should be writing the contents of the selected address to Q0–Q3.

options.label: D
options.text: The Q0–Q3 lines can be either LOW or HIGH, since the chip is in the tristate mode in which case their level is unpredictable.

stem: What is the meaning of RAM, and what is its primary role?

options.label: A
options.text: Readily Available Memory; it is the first level of memory used by the computer in all of its operations.

options.label: B
options.text: Random Access Memory; it is memory that can be reached by any sub-system within a computer, and at any time.

options.label: C
options.text: Random Access Memory; it is the memory used for short-term temporary data storage within the computer.

options.label: D
options.text: Resettable Automatic Memory; it is memory that can be used and then automatically reset, or cleared, after being read from or written to.

stem: ______ are used to overcome the difference in data transfer speeds of various devices.
explanation: By using Buffer registers, the processor sends the data to the I/O device at the processor speed and the data gets stored in the buffer. After that the data gets sent to or from the buffer to the devices at the device speed.

stem: The term "FAT" is stands for_____
Which one of the following is not true?

options.label: A
options.text: kernel remains in the memory during the entire computer session

options.label: B
options.text: kernel is made of various modules which can not be loaded in running operating system

options.label: C
options.text: kernel is the first part of the operating system to load into memory during booting

options.label: D
options.text: kernel is the program that constitutes the central core of the operating system

explanation: Kernel is the first program that is loaded in memory when OS is loading as well as it remains in memory till OS is running. Kernel is the core part of the OS which is responsible for managing resources, allowing multiple processes to use the resources and provide services to various processes. Kernel modules can be loaded and unloaded in run-time i.e. in running OS.

stem: What does OS X has?
explanation: OS X has a hybrid kernel. Hybrid kernel is a combination of two different kernels. OS X is developed by Apple and originally it is known as Mac OS X.
